key_risks: Hot May PCE print on 6/25 could reverse the duration and small-cap rally
Mega-cap tech rebound would lift SPY benchmark and hurt relative performance of equal-weight tilt
Sticky CPI (4.2% YoY headline) limits Fed easing optionality
Small/mid-cap reversal risk after strong recent run into 52w highs
Holiday-shortened week with July 3 closure may amplify volatility around exit

portfolio.rationale: Strong breadth participation with positive 30d benchmark-relative return; supported by Russell 2000 and equal-weight strength signaling rotation away from mega-cap tech.
Housing-related strength (KB Home +16.7%, DRH +6.7%) and falling 10Y yield (4.40%) support small value; at 52w high with positive catalysts.
Manufacturing PMI at 55.7 and output at 57.7 (strong expansion); benefits from cyclical rotation visible in Dow outperformance.
Defensive sector with positive momentum; biotech strength (XBI +7.5% 7d) and low beta provide ballast against tech weakness.
10Y yield dropped to 4.40% from 4.50%; falling oil/gold suggests disinflation impulse benefiting duration into PCE data on 6/25.

portfolio_rationale: Tilts toward broadening market rotation away from mega-cap tech (Microsoft -2.3%, Oracle -4.6%) into mid/small caps, industrials, and rate-sensitive housing-linked names, with healthcare defense and duration hedge as yields decline ahead of PCE.
rationale_summary: Cyclical broadening trade with defensive healthcare and duration hedge, capitalizing on falling yields, strong manufacturing PMI, and visible rotation under the index surface.
